projects.title: Intelligent Blood Donor Locator
projects.subtitle: Location-Aware Emergency Web Application
projects.desc: Engineered a full-stack, location-aware web application designed to dramatically reduce response time during blood donation emergencies. The system leverages OPTICS (Ordering Points To Identify the Clustering Structure) — a density-based clustering algorithm — combined with real-time geospatial distance filtering to intelligently rank and surface the most geographically and medically relevant donors for a given request. The application was architected with a robust role-based access control system, supporting three distinct user personas: admin, hospitals, and donors — each with dedicated dashboards, workflows, and permission levels. Real-time donor request and response mechanisms were implemented to minimize latency between request creation and donor notification. Additionally, the platform features automated certificate generation for registered donors using image processing techniques, enhancing user engagement and institutional trust. The responsive UI ensures seamless usability across both desktop and mobile devices, making the system accessible during high-pressure emergency scenarios.

projects.title: Seoul Bike Rental Prediction
projects.subtitle: ML-Powered Demand Forecasting System
projects.desc: Developed an end-to-end machine learning pipeline to forecast hourly bike rental demand in Seoul using a rich dataset of environmental, temporal, and operational variables. The project began with extensive Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) — including correlation heatmaps, distribution analysis, seasonal trend visualization, and outlier detection — to deeply understand the dataset before any modeling. A thorough data preprocessing stage followed, leveraging Scikit-learn pipelines for feature selection, scaling, and encoding to ensure clean, model-ready input. The core model — a Random Forest Regressor — was selected for its robustness to non-linear relationships and resistance to overfitting, then fine-tuned through hyperparameter optimization to maximize forecasting accuracy. The final model delivered strong predictive performance, offering actionable operational insights such as peak demand periods, weather impact on rentals, and holiday effects — insights directly applicable to fleet management and resource planning for bike-sharing operators.

projects.title: PMFC-Aeroponics System
projects.subtitle: Green Tech Sustainable Energy & Agriculture Innovation
projects.desc: Conceptualized, designed, and engineered an innovative integrated system that merges three cutting-edge sustainable technologies: Plant Microbial Fuel Cells (PMFC), solar energy harvesting, and IoT-driven automation. The PMFC component harnesses the natural electrochemical activity of soil microorganisms around plant roots to generate bio-electricity, while solar panels provide supplementary renewable energy — together powering the entire aeroponics farming setup without reliance on external grid electricity. The aeroponics system itself delivers nutrient-rich mist directly to plant root zones suspended in air, dramatically reducing water consumption compared to traditional soil or hydroponic farming. IoT-based sensor automation monitors and regulates nutrient delivery, humidity, light cycles, and power generation in real time, enabling a fully self-sustaining and remotely manageable agricultural unit. The project demonstrated meaningful potential for decentralized food production and clean energy generation, particularly for resource-constrained or off-grid environments.
